reviewer_stances.reason: The most detailed and substantiated review. Its central weakness (the benchmark is dominated by spatially-structured sensor networks and lacks weakly-correlated/heterogeneous datasets, with Figure 5(f) showing the advantage shrinking at low correlation) directly matches the meta-review's anchor concern about the datasets used. Its novelty-positioning and mixed-ablation (multi-level fusion) points are independently corroborated by other reviewers.

Specific and substantiated. The novelty-distinction concern (Crossformer-style two-stage attention, prior learnable per-series embeddings) and the dataset/missingness-diversity concern are corroborated by other reviewers, and the concrete ablation observations (gated fusion degrading ~10.8% on average, ~34.6% on BeijingAir) are precise and evaluation-relevant.

Thoughtful and specific on the FeatID-attribution concern (embeddings optimized only indirectly through the imputation loss, possibly absorbing dataset-specific shortcuts; loss-weighting sensitivity), which is corroborated by Reviewer_8VMy, and on architectural complexity not being consistently justified by the ablations. Somewhat narrower in scope than the two high-weight reviews.

On-target on transferability (dataset-specific FeatID) and on the theoretical justification being largely qualitative, both corroborated by others. However, several suggestions (uncertainty quantification, scalability to F>10^3, adding diffusion baselines) are more generic wishlist items not echoed by the meta-review, so its distinctive points carry less weight.

score_rationale: The meta-review anchors on the datasets used as the principal concern and on compelling empirical results as the decisive positive, so I foregrounded the dataset-composition weakness (raised most sharply by the high-weight Reviewer_y3Tu and corroborated by Reviewer_2aSB) while crediting the strong, thorough empirics. Soundness, significance, and originality are fixed at 3 (unanimous across reviewers); presentation is 3, following the two high-weight reviewers rather than the single 4 from a medium-weight reviewer. Overall recommendation is 4, the lower end of the [4,5] reviewer range, reflecting an honest pre-rebuttal position with the dataset-generalization and FeatID-attribution concerns still open, while remaining clearly on the accept side consistent with the meta-review's positive direction. Confidence is 4 given the strong mutual corroboration across reviewers on datasets, novelty positioning, mixed ablations, and FeatID attribution.
